idea-plugin.name: Patchwork Bridge
idea-plugin.description:
Patchwork Bridge for JetBrains IDEs

Connects IntelliJ IDEA (and other JetBrains IDEs) to the Patchwork OS bridge, giving Claude Code and other AI assistants live access to your IDE state over the MCP protocol.

What this plugin does

Once installed, your running Patchwork bridge can ask IntelliJ to:

  • Read editor state — current selection, open files, file content, dirty state
  • Navigate — go to definition, declaration, type definition, find references and implementations
  • Inspect — hover documentation, document symbols, call hierarchy, workspace symbol search
  • Edit — apply text edits, replace blocks, create/rename/delete files
  • Refactor — rename symbols, prepare rename, format document, organize imports, fix lint errors
  • Diagnostics — read errors and warnings across the workspace or per file
  • Terminal — list, create, dispose terminals; send commands; run and capture command output
  • Debug — read debug state, set breakpoints, evaluate expressions, start/stop sessions
  • Clipboard — read and write the system clipboard

49 handlers in total. Wire-compatible with the VS Code companion extension — the same Claude Code MCP tools work against both IDEs without any AI-side changes.

Requirements

  • Patchwork bridge running locally (patchwork start or npm start)
  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 or later (Community or Ultimate). Also works in Android Studio, GoLand, PyCharm, Rider, RubyMine, WebStorm — any 2024.1+ JetBrains IDE.

How it works

The plugin reads the bridge lock file at ~/.claude/ide/<port>.lock, opens a WebSocket to the local bridge process, and handles JSON-RPC 2.0 requests. No data leaves your machine. No cloud accounts required.

Protocol version

1.1.0 — compatible with Patchwork bridge v0.2.0-alpha.18 and later.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0 — 2026-04-22

Initial release. 49 real handlers across five groups:

  • Core (23): selection, open files, file content, open/save/close, create/delete/rename, editText, replaceBlock, clipboard, diagnostics, workspace folders, isDirty
  • LSP (18): goToDefinition/Declaration/TypeDefinition, findReferences/Implementations, getHover, getCodeActions, renameSymbol, searchSymbols, getDocumentSymbols, getCallHierarchy, prepareRename, formatRange, signatureHelp, foldingRanges, selectionRanges
  • Debug (5): getDebugState, evaluateInDebugger, setDebugBreakpoints, startDebugging, stopDebugging
  • Terminal (7): listTerminals, createTerminal, disposeTerminal, sendTerminalCommand, getTerminalOutput, waitForTerminalOutput, executeInTerminal
  • Code style (3): formatDocument, organizeImports, fixAllLintErrors
